page.title: How to Hire a General Counsel in the UK
page.intro:
The General Counsel is the most senior legal officer in the organisation, responsible for legal strategy, risk management, compliance, and corporate governance. The role has expanded significantly — modern GCs are expected to be commercial business partners, not just legal advisors. Finding someone with both legal excellence and commercial credibility is the central challenge.

The evolution of the GC role reflects the growing complexity of the legal and regulatory environment in which UK organisations operate. Employment law, data protection, sanctions compliance, ESG-linked disclosure requirements, and sector-specific regulation have all increased in complexity over the last decade. The GC must have the technical depth to manage these requirements and the strategic perspective to advise the board on legal risk in commercial terms.

In listed companies, the GC often has a dual role as Company Secretary, with responsibility for board governance, regulatory filings, and shareholder relations. This requires a different profile from a pure legal advisory role — one that combines legal expertise with governance knowledge and stakeholder management skills.
considerations: Define the legal complexity of the business — regulated industries, international operations, M&A activity, and litigation history all shape the required profile.
Clarify whether this is a standalone GC or the head of a legal team — the leadership and management requirements differ significantly.
Establish the GC's relationship with the board and audit committee — direct board access is a prerequisite for effective legal leadership.
Specify compliance and regulatory requirements — sector-specific knowledge (FCA, ICO, CMA) can be essential and significantly narrows the candidate pool.
Consider whether external law firm experience or an in-house track record is the priority — both have merits, and the balance depends on the organisation's legal model.
page.salaryRange: £95,000–£240,000 depending on organisation complexity and sector
page.timeline: 10–14 weeks from briefing to accepted offer
page.whySearch: The best General Counsels combine deep legal expertise with commercial pragmatism, leadership capability, and board-level credibility. This combination is rare and these candidates are highly valued by their current employers. A headhunter who understands the legal market and has relationships with in-house legal professionals can identify and approach the candidates who are genuinely right for the role.
